summary: Every membership row can determine an employee's selected department, so exact reporting requires a linear scan; one conditional hash aggregation pass matches that lower bound.

optimality.argument: Any membership row may be an employee's only row or the row marked as primary, so omitting it can change the output. An exact algorithm must therefore inspect all R rows. Grouped conditional aggregation processes each row once and maintains one state per employee, giving O(R) expected logical time.

replacement_checks.evidence: Changing or removing any uninspected row can change an implicit single-department result or the explicit primary department.
One hash aggregation pass updates a conditional primary maximum and a fallback department maximum for exactly one employee group per source row.

Authored fixtures cover single implicit departments, explicit primaries before and after other rows, a primary smaller than nonprimary department IDs, interleaved employees, unsorted IDs, and mixed membership counts.
